name: Ably Realtime API
what_it_does: Provides globally distributed pub/sub messaging infrastructure with channels, presence tracking, and guaranteed message ordering for real-time applications.
use_cases: Broadcasting agent status updates and task progress to subscribed clients via channels
Coordinating multi-agent pipelines where agents publish events to trigger downstream agents
Maintaining live presence state for which agents or users are currently active in a session
Streaming LLM output tokens from backend agents to frontend clients in real time
Implementing agent-to-agent command and control messaging with delivery guarantees
not_for: Durable persistent storage — messages are transient and not queryable after delivery
Request/response RPC patterns — Ably is push-based and not designed for synchronous calls
Replacing a task queue or job broker — no built-in retry, scheduling, or dead-letter queues
best_when: You need globally low-latency pub/sub with presence, guaranteed ordering, and connection state recovery for real-time agent or user coordination.
avoid_when: You need messages to persist indefinitely and be replayed on demand, or require synchronous request/response semantics.

auth.notes: API keys support capability-based scoping per channel and operation. Token requests allow short-lived delegated credentials, which is the recommended pattern for client-side agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 6 million messages/month, 200 peak concurrent connections, 100 channels
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from ~$29/month; Scale and custom enterprise plans available
pricing.notes: Pricing scales on messages and peak connections. Reactor integrations (webhooks, serverless) count as messages.

performance.rate_limits: Publish rate limited per API key; default 50 messages/second per channel; configurable on paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK errors are descriptive with error codes and reason strings. REST API errors follow standard HTTP codes with JSON bodies. Connection state change events expose reason codes useful for automated retry logic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Messages can carry a clientId and unique ID but the platform does not natively deduplicate publishes. Agents must implement their own idempotency keys if exactly-once semantics are required.
known_agent_gotchas: Connection state machine has multiple states (connecting, connected, disconnected, suspended, closed) — agents must handle all states, not just connected/disconnected
Token expiry mid-session will silently drop messages unless the agent implements an authCallback to refresh tokens
Channel reattachment after network interruption is automatic but messages published during disconnect may be missed unless the agent uses rewind or channel history
Presence enter/leave events can fire multiple times during reconnects, causing agents to double-count active members
REST publish and Realtime SDK publish have different rate limit buckets — mixing both in one agent can cause unexpected throttling

security_score_components.security_notes: Capability-scoped tokens are a strong pattern. API keys should be server-side only; short-lived token requests are the correct client pattern. Ably supports mutual TLS on enterprise plans.
